title: Tilington plc Cloud Modernisation Programme

shared_files.title: Company Background Document
shared_files.description: Tilington plc background, CTO mandate, £3.2M budget, regulatory context (FCA, PRA, UK GDPR), stakeholders, and programme timeline to Q3 2027 DC exit.

shared_files.title: Server Inventory — 90 Servers
shared_files.description: Full inventory of all 90 servers across DC1 London and DC2 Manchester. Contains OS versions, EOL status, patch status, and database assignments. Includes two undocumented servers (SRV-086/087), a shared Oracle instance on SRV-076, and multiple CRITICAL patch status entries.

shared_files.title: Application Inventory — 28 Applications
shared_files.description: All 28 Tilington applications with 6R migration classification, business owners, database dependencies, and regulatory scope. APP-027 status is deliberately left unclear for the candidate to investigate.

shared_files.title: Application Dependency Map
shared_files.description: 68 documented and undocumented application dependencies. Contains deliberate traps: APP-006 depends on MSSQL-03 which is not in the server inventory, and APP-002 uses an undocumented SMB file share with APP-003.

shared_files.title: AWS Cost Report — January 2026
shared_files.description: January 2026 AWS spend across 4 accounts. 45 rows. Contains the cross-AZ wrong-direction trap: CloudWatch DataProcessing at $89.40 looks like the problem, but Data Transfer Regional at $156.30 is the real architectural issue fixable with free VPC gateway endpoints.

shared_files.title: Security Findings — 20 Findings
shared_files.description: 20 Security Hub and GuardDuty findings. Includes SF-002 and SF-018 (public S3 bucket with customer NI numbers — potential ICO notification) and SF-006 (active SSH brute force from Tor exit node).

shared_files.title: Migration Wave Candidates
shared_files.description: All 28 apps with recommended migration wave, complexity rating, sequencing rationale, and pre-migration tasks. APP-012 (Authentication Service) must be Wave 2 — 25 apps depend on it.

shared_files.title: Starter GitHub Repository
shared_files.description: 15-20% complete starter repository. Contains incomplete Terraform modules, a broken GitHub Actions pipeline with 10 documented bugs, partial Kubernetes manifests, and Python automation tool starters.

company.name: Tilington plc
company.brief: Tilington plc is a UK-regulated financial services firm (asset management + retail banking, 1,200 staff) migrating from two on-premise datacentres to AWS. Both DC leases expire Q3 2027 — a hard deadline. The candidate acts as Lead AWS Solutions Architect responsible for designing a scalable, secure, reliable AWS platform with microservices, Kubernetes, CI/CD, observability, migration planning, DR, and cost optimisation.

personas.bio: Sarah approved the £3.2M programme budget personally and presented it to the Board. She is accountable for delivery and has political capital invested in success. She will not review your Terraform — she will read your one-page executive summary and ask three pointed questions. Expects outcomes, not diagrams.

inbox_messages.subject: Welcome to the Tilington Cloud Programme
inbox_messages.body:
Welcome aboard. I'll be direct: this programme has a hard deadline and I need it to succeed.

Your first priority is discovery. I need to understand what we're dealing with before I commit publicly to the Oracle renewal decision in Q2. Michael will brief you on the estate — but I warn you, the documentation is patchy. Don't trust the CMDB without verifying it.

I'd like to see your initial findings by the end of Phase 2. Please structure your report so I can hand the executive summary to the Board without editing it.

One request: if you find anything that changes the programme timeline or budget, tell me immediately.

Sarah

inbox_messages.subject: ARB Date — 14 April
inbox_messages.body:
The Architecture Review Board is confirmed for 14 April. You will present your target architecture and answer questions from Emma Clarke (risk), Priya Sharma (security), and David Okafor (retail technology).

Emma is particularly focused on the data residency question — she wants written confirmation that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario, including the DR failover to eu-west-1.

The Board CFO will also be in the room. He will ask about cost. Have your numbers ready.

Sarah

inbox_messages.subject: Oracle Renewal — Decision Needed This Week
inbox_messages.body:
Our Oracle renewal window closes 30 April. I need your recommendation: do we renew or not renew?

The CFO wants a business case. If we don't renew, what's the risk? If we do renew, what does that cost us and what does it signal to the programme?

I need a clear recommendation with numbers by Thursday.

Sarah

personas.bio: Michael built DC1 in 2014 and DC2 in 2016. He knows every server in the estate — or thought he did until two undocumented servers turned up in the physical audit. He is your closest technical ally but will push back on anything unsafe. Has AWS Solutions Architect Associate but has never used Terraform in production.

inbox_messages.subject: Things you need to know before you start
inbox_messages.body:
Hey — welcome. I've pushed the server inventory to the repo but fair warning: it's not complete.

We found two servers in DC1 Rack 21 during the physical audit last week that aren't in the CMDB. SRV-086 and SRV-087. Nobody owns them. Could be test boxes from 2022 or could be something important.

Also — APP-027 (Legacy Fund Accounting). Finance say they stopped using it 18 months ago. But I'm still seeing database sessions on ORCL-05 that look like it's still connecting. Can you help me nail down whether this is active or not?

One more thing: APP-006 references MSSQL-03 in the dependency map but I cannot find MSSQL-03 anywhere in the server inventory.

Michael

inbox_messages.subject: Oracle — We Need to Talk
inbox_messages.body:
I need to flag something before it becomes a problem.

ORCL-01 has 847 stored procedures. I've done a rough pass with the Oracle Schema Conversion Tool and it's flagging 214 of them as requiring manual conversion. Some of them are PL/SQL packages that took our DBA six months to write. The most complex is customer_ledger_pkg.

ORCL-02 is even more complex — it has custom scheduler jobs and 3 database links to external systems (SWIFT, Refinitiv, a legacy FTP gateway).

I'm not saying we can't do it. I'm saying Sarah's timeline assumes this is straightforward and it isn't. Can you factor this into your Phase 8 database planning?

Michael

personas.bio: Emma spent nine years as a senior supervisor at the FCA before joining Tilington. She knows how the regulator thinks because she used to be one. She will not approve a production migration until she has evidence in writing. She can stop the programme. Cites regulations by section. Expects specific technical mechanisms — not prose assertions.

inbox_messages.subject: Regulatory and Compliance Requirements
inbox_messages.body:
I have reviewed the programme brief. I want to set out my requirements clearly so there are no surprises at the ARB.

Before any Tilington customer data is processed on AWS infrastructure, I require: written confirmation of data residency controls (UK GDPR Article 44), FCA material outsourcing notification filed and acknowledged (FCA SYSC 8), and AWS CloudTrail enabled with immutable logs in a separate logging account with 7-year retention.

These are not negotiable. They are regulatory requirements, not preferences.

Emma Clarke
Chief Risk Officer

inbox_messages.subject: URGENT — S3 Public Bucket and PII Exposure
inbox_messages.body:
I have been made aware of findings SF-002 and SF-018.

We have a production S3 bucket (tilington-temp-uploads-prod) that is publicly accessible and contains objects in which Macie has detected National Insurance numbers.

I need within 24 hours: confirmation that public access has been blocked, a data lineage note explaining how NI numbers ended up in this bucket, and a preliminary assessment of whether ICO notification is required under UK GDPR Article 33.

This is a potential data breach. I am notifying the CEO.

Emma Clarke

inbox_messages.subject: ARB Pack — Data Residency and DR Policy Questions
inbox_messages.body:
For the ARB on 14 April, I will be asking the following questions. Prepare written answers in your ARB pack.

Question 1: Confirm that during DR failover to eu-west-1, no customer PII is replicated to or processed in that region. What technical controls enforce this? What happens if the control fails?

Question 2: How does your architecture maintain operational resilience if AWS eu-west-2 experiences an availability event?

Question 3: Who within Tilington has administrative access to the AWS Production account? How is privileged access managed and audited?

I expect specific, evidenced answers — not architectural assertions.

Emma Clarke

personas.bio: James joined two years ago to lead the cloud journey. AWS Solutions Architect Professional certified. Has run EKS in production for three years. Your primary technical contact and most likely to review your pull requests. Strong opinions on GitOps — considers ClickOps a professional failing. Prefers Slack to email.

inbox_messages.subject: Repo access and things to know
inbox_messages.body:
Hey! Welcome. I've set you up with read access to the AWS accounts. Repo is at github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation.

Quick heads up: the Terraform networking module is about 20% done — private subnets and NAT GWs are commented out TODOs. The GitHub Actions pipeline has 10 bugs I deliberately left in for you to find and fix. The ArgoCD application.yaml is pointing at a cluster URL that doesn't exist yet.

Also: check the January cost report in migration/aws_cost_report.csv. There's something that looks like a monitoring cost problem but I think is actually an architectural problem. See if you spot it.

James

inbox_messages.subject: January Cost Report — Something Looks Wrong
inbox_messages.body:
I was reviewing the January cost report and something caught my eye.

CloudWatch DataProcessing is $89.40. At first I thought it was a monitoring verbosity issue.

But then I noticed AWS Data Transfer Regional is $156.30. That's cross-AZ data transfer. For a cluster the size of ours that seems high.

My theory: something is making a lot of cross-AZ calls that should be using VPC endpoints. It's architectural, not a monitoring config problem.

Can you investigate? If I'm right, VPC gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB should fix it — and they're free.

James

inbox_messages.subject: Architecture decisions we need ADRs for
inbox_messages.body:
A few things we need Architecture Decision Records for before the ARB:

1. eu-west-2 as primary region — needs to be written down with the UK GDPR rationale
2. Terraform as primary IaC — I want this locked in before anyone suggests CloudFormation-only
3. EKS vs ECS — I've committed us to EKS but we need the documented trade-off

Also the NAT Gateway question. Do we run 1 NAT GW (cost) or 3 (one per AZ, HA)? About £90/month per additional NAT GW. Document it as an ADR.

James

eva.simulation_context: Tilington plc is a UK financial services firm migrating 90 servers and 28 applications from two on-premise datacentres to AWS before Q3 2027 lease expiry. The candidate is the Lead AWS Solutions Architect responsible for discovery, architecture, IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, database design, migration planning, and board-level governance across 9 phases.
eva.answer_key_summary: Key figures: 14 EOL OS servers, SRV-086/087 undocumented, SRV-076 hosts ORCL-04+ORCL-05, MSSQL-03 not in inventory, APP-027 status unclear. January AWS spend ~$10,100 USD. Cross-AZ trap: Data Transfer Regional $156.30 is the real issue (not CloudWatch $89.40) — fix with free S3+DynamoDB VPC gateway endpoints saving $1,875.60/year. AuditTrail DynamoDB must have NO TTL (FCA). Payments Service 15-min RPO requires Aurora PITR specifically.

phases.eva_context: The candidate is conducting discovery of Tilington's legacy estate. Key messy reality items: SRV-086/087 undocumented servers, APP-027 unclear status, MSSQL-03 ghost reference, ORCL-04/05 sharing SRV-076.
phases.eva_guidance: Help the candidate understand what to look for in the CSV files. If they ask about APP-027, remind them to check both Finance's claim (decommissioned) and Michael's observation (active DB sessions). Do not give away the answer — guide them to investigate.
common_questions: How do I classify an application using 6Rs?
What does EOL OS mean for the risk register?
How do I find MSSQL-03?

phases.eva_context: The candidate is designing the target AWS architecture and landing zone. ADR-001 and ADR-002 are already accepted. They need to write SCPs in JSON and design IAM Identity Centre with Azure AD federation.
phases.eva_guidance: Help with SCP JSON syntax if asked. Remind them Emma Clarke needs a written data residency mechanism — not a diagram. If they ask about EKS vs ECS, prompt them to document the trade-off in ADR-005.
common_questions: How do I write an SCP to deny public S3 buckets?
How does IAM Identity Centre federate with Azure AD?
What goes in the LLD vs HLD?

phases.eva_context: Networking, security, and hybrid cloud. The cross-AZ cost trap is active here — CloudWatch $89.40 looks like the problem but Data Transfer Regional $156.30 is architectural and fixed by free VPC gateway endpoints.
phases.eva_guidance: If the candidate focuses on CloudWatch costs, ask them to look at the Data Transfer Regional line in the cost report. Do not tell them the answer directly. For SF-002/SF-018, remind them Emma Clarke mentioned a 72-hour ICO notification clock.
common_questions: What is a VPC gateway endpoint and is it free?
How do I fix SF-004 without SSH?
What is the NCSC 14 Cloud Security Principles?

phases.eva_context: IaC, automation, and operations. The candidate must complete Terraform modules, Python tools, and CloudFormation templates. Three incidents to resolve.
phases.eva_guidance: If they ask about Object Lock COMPLIANCE vs GOVERNANCE: COMPLIANCE means not even the root account can delete logs before the retention period expires — this is what FCA audit requires. GOVERNANCE allows admin override. Help with Terraform syntax if stuck but do not write the code for them.
common_questions: What is the difference between Object Lock COMPLIANCE and GOVERNANCE?
How do I run terraform plan for a specific variable?
How do I make the Python Boto3 inventory tool identify Oracle RDS instances?

phases.eva_context: Kubernetes, microservices, and GitOps. Four microservices to deploy to EKS. Helm chart templates to create. ArgoCD ApplicationSet to configure.
phases.eva_guidance: If they ask about PCI DSS isolation for the Payments Service: a separate Kubernetes namespace with NetworkPolicy restricting inbound is the correct approach. Remind them every ServiceAccount needs the IRSA annotation. If helm template fails, check for missing required values in values.yaml.
common_questions: What is IRSA and why does every ServiceAccount need it?
How do I write a Helm deployment template that uses values.yaml?
What is an ArgoCD ApplicationSet?

phases.eva_context: CI/CD, DevSecOps, and observability. 10 bugs to find and fix in the GitHub Actions pipeline. BUG-004 is the most subtle — the security-scan job is skipped on non-main branches and returns success, allowing downstream jobs to proceed.
phases.eva_guidance: For BUG-004: ask the candidate what happens to the `needs:` dependency when a job is skipped due to an `if:` condition. The answer is it counts as passed, not blocked. For the SLO error budget: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = 21.6 minutes. Guide them to show their working.
common_questions: Why does BUG-004 cause the security scan to never block the pipeline?
How do I calculate the Payments Service error budget?
What is the difference between a fast burn and slow burn alert?

phases.eva_context: Database architecture. Aurora PostgreSQL replaces Oracle. DynamoDB for event data. AuditTrail must have NO TTL. Payments Service needs 15-minute RPO via PITR specifically.
phases.eva_guidance: If they set TTL on AuditTrail: explain FCA COBS record-keeping — some records must be retained indefinitely, so TTL is not permitted. For the 15-minute RPO: daily backups give 24-hour RPO, PITR gives sub-minute RPO — only PITR achieves 15 minutes. For the hot partition problem on PaymentEvents: guide them to write sharding.
common_questions: Why can't AuditTrail have a TTL?
How does Aurora PITR achieve a 15-minute RPO?
What is write sharding in DynamoDB?

phases.eva_context: Migration, DR, and FinOps. The January total spend must be calculated from the CSV — not estimated. AuditTrail must not replicate to eu-west-1. The cross-AZ VPC endpoint fix saves $1,875.60/year.
phases.eva_guidance: If they estimate the January total instead of calculating from the CSV, remind them the rubric requires the calculated sum. For the DR architecture: if they propose Aurora Global Tables for customer data, remind them that would put PII in eu-west-1 which violates UK GDPR.
common_questions: How do I sum the blended_cost_usd column?
Why can't Aurora customer data replicate to eu-west-1?
What is the annual saving from VPC gateway endpoints?

phases.eva_context: Architecture Review Board and executive presentation. Emma Clarke has three specific questions that must be answered in writing with named technical mechanisms.
phases.eva_guidance: For Emma Clarke's data residency question: the answer must name the SCP preventing eu-west-1 PII use, the CloudTrail detective control, and the Aurora snapshot-only strategy. Prose assertions like 'we have controls in place' will fail. Help the candidate be specific.
common_questions: What technical mechanism enforces the data residency constraint during DR?
How do I explain the error budget to the Board CFO?
What should go in the risk and trade-off summary?

phases.eva_context: Completion phase. The candidate has finished all 9 phases.
phases.eva_guidance: Congratulate the candidate. Help them reflect on the key takeaways and how their portfolio maps to UK financial services AWS Solutions Architect roles paying £70,000–£125,000.
common_questions: How do I present this portfolio to a recruiter?
Which phase deliverables are most important for interviews?

description: Tilington plc is a UK financial services firm (asset management and retail banking, 1,200 staff) migrating 90 servers and 28 applications from two on-premise datacentres to AWS before a hard Q3 2027 lease expiry deadline. You are the Lead AWS Solutions Architect. Across 9 phases you will conduct enterprise discovery of a messy legacy estate, design a multi-account AWS landing zone, build Terraform IaC modules, deploy four microservices to EKS using GitOps, build a DevSecOps CI/CD pipeline, design Aurora PostgreSQL and DynamoDB data architectures, plan a phased migration and DR strategy, and present your full architecture to an executive Architecture Review Board. The estate contains deliberate complexity: undocumented servers, unclear application status, shared Oracle instances, a broken pipeline with 10 bugs to find and fix, and a FinOps wrong-direction trap. Every deliverable is pushed to a real GitHub repository — your portfolio when complete targets AWS Solutions Architect roles in UK financial services paying £70,000–£125,000.

phases.title: Welcome to Tilington plc
phases.pre_phase_context: You have just been appointed Lead AWS Solutions Architect at Tilington plc. The Board has approved a £3.2M programme to migrate 90 servers and 28 applications to AWS before both datacentre leases expire in Q3 2027. Start by reading the company brief, meeting your team, and setting up the GitHub repository that will hold your portfolio.
objectives: Understand Tilington's business context, regulatory environment, and programme drivers
Meet your four key stakeholders and understand their priorities and potential conflicts
Set up the starter GitHub repository that will hold all your deliverables
Understand the data files and deliberate messy-reality elements you will encounter
skills_preview: Regulatory context (FCA, UK GDPR, PCI DSS)

business_impact.without_this: You start technical work without understanding the regulatory constraints or stakeholder dynamics, leading to architecture decisions that Emma Clarke will block
business_impact.with_this: You enter Phase 2 with full situational awareness — understanding why eu-west-1 cannot hold PII, why the Oracle renewal is urgent, and what each stakeholder will scrutinise

content.text:
## About Tilington plc

Tilington plc operates two business lines:

- **Tilington Asset Management** — institutional asset management, £4.2bn AUM

- **Tilington Retail Financial Services** — ISAs, savings, and investment products for approximately 180,000 individual customers

Offices in London (head office), Manchester (operations), and Edinburgh (fund management). 1,200 employees. Regulated by the FCA and PRA.

---

## Why This Programme Exists

Three drivers make this migration urgent — not optional:

**Driver 1 — Datacentre Lease Expiry (Hard Deadline)**

Both DC leases expire Q3 2027. No renewal option. All workloads must migrate or decommission.

**Driver 2 — FCA Operational Resilience (Active Enforcement)**

FCA PS21/3 required firms to set and test impact tolerances by March 2025. Tilington is in remediation. Cloud migration is the primary fix.

**Driver 3 — Oracle Licensing Cost**

Oracle contract: £1.4M per year. 3-year renewal due Q2 2026. Target: migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL and eliminate this cost. Potential saving: £4.2M over 3 years.

---

## Programme Budget

Board-approved budget: £3,200,000 over 18 months — approximately £56,667 per month.

content.caption: UK GDPR constraint: All customer personal data must remain in eu-west-2 at all times — including during DR failover to eu-west-1.

content.intro: You will work with four key stakeholders throughout this programme. Each has different priorities — and sometimes conflicting ones. Understanding who they are and what they care about is essential to navigating this project.

content.text:
## Stakeholder Conflicts to Be Aware Of

These conflicts are real and will surface throughout the programme. You will need to navigate them.

**Sarah vs Emma — Speed vs Compliance**

Sarah wants to move fast. Emma requires written controls and evidence before any production migration. Emma has veto power. Sarah has budget authority. You are in the middle.

**Sarah vs Michael — Timeline Realism**

Sarah set an 18-month timeline. Michael privately thinks it is too tight, particularly given the Oracle PL/SQL complexity (847 stored procedures, 214 requiring manual conversion). He raises this in private but softens it in programme calls.

**James vs Emma — Dev Data**

James wanted to use production-like customer data in the dev environment for testing. Emma blocked it on UK GDPR grounds. James complied but uses synthetic data. This means testing is less representative.

**Michael vs James — Rehost vs Refactor**

Michael wants to rehost workloads quickly and refactor later. James wants cloud-native from Day 1. The simulation requires you to make an ADR on this trade-off.

content.instruction: Read the Company Background Document first, then clone or bookmark the GitHub repository. You will commit all your deliverables to the repo throughout this programme.

content.text:
## Your GitHub Repository

The starter repository is at **github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation**

This repository is 15-20% complete by design. Your job across 9 phases is to complete it.

- Files marked `# TODO:` need your work

- Files marked `# STARTER` show you the pattern — extend them

- Files with `???` are your architectural decisions to make

- Do not delete starter files. Build on them.

---

## Data Files in /migration/

Six CSV files drive the simulation. Read them carefully before Phase 2.

Each file contains deliberate complexity and hidden traps that you must surface during discovery.

rows: CloudWatch $89.40 is a distraction — Data Transfer $156.30 is the real issue

content.question: Tilington has chosen eu-west-1 (Ireland) as its DR region. Which of the following is permitted in eu-west-1 under the programme's data residency policy?
options: Aurora PostgreSQL read replicas containing customer account data
DynamoDB Global Table replication of the AuditTrail table
Non-PII configuration data and infrastructure state
Encrypted backups of customer payment history

rubric.criterion_label: Correctly identifies that only non-PII data is permitted in eu-west-1 (DR region) under UK GDPR

content.headline: Before you can design anything, you need to understand what Tilington actually has.
content.description: Before you can design anything, you need to understand what Tilington actually has. The estate is messier than anyone admits — two servers are not in the CMDB, one application may or may not still be running, and a database referenced in the dependency map does not exist in the server inventory.

content.text:
## Discovery & Current State Assessment

**Portfolio output:** Enterprise Discovery Pack

**GitHub evidence:** /docs/discovery-report.md, /architecture/current-state.drawio, /migration/application-inventory.xlsx, /docs/stakeholder-register.md

**Tools:** Draw.io (app.diagrams.net — free), Google Sheets or Excel, GitHub

**Estimated time:** 6–10 hours

---

## What Makes This Phase Difficult

The estate has four deliberate messy-reality elements:

- **SRV-086 and SRV-087** — discovered in DC1 Rack 21 during a physical audit. Not in the CMDB. Nobody knows what they are.

- **APP-027** — Finance says decommissioned 18 months ago. Michael Evans sees active database sessions on ORCL-05. One of them is wrong.

- **MSSQL-03** — referenced in the dependency map by APP-006, but does not appear anywhere in the server inventory.

- **SRV-076** — hosts both ORCL-04 and ORCL-05 on the same physical server in DC2. This is undocumented.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Discovery Assessment Report

**Commit to:** `/docs/discovery-report.md`

Write a structured report covering:

**Executive Summary** — board-readable. Current state in 3–4 bullets. Top 3 risks. Is the 18-month timeline achievable? Be honest.

**Infrastructure Assessment** — how many servers are on EOL OS? List them by server ID. How many have CRITICAL patch status? What is the VMware EOL risk? Risk assessment for SRV-086 and SRV-087.

**Application Portfolio Assessment** — classify all 28 applications using 6Rs. For APP-027 (Legacy Fund Accounting): Finance says decommissioned 18 months ago, Michael sees active DB sessions on ORCL-05. Investigate and give a definitive recommendation.

**Dependency Analysis** — APP-006 references MSSQL-03 in the dependency map, but MSSQL-03 does not appear in the server inventory. What is your finding and recommendation? Note: SRV-076 in DC2 appears to host both ORCL-04 and ORCL-05 on the same physical server — this is undocumented.

**Compliance Gap Analysis** — EOL OS servers and FCA/ISO 27001 implications. CMDB accuracy risk. Documentation coverage risk.

**Risk Register Extension** — add at least 5 new risks to the company brief risk register. Each must have: description, impact, likelihood, owner, mitigation.

---

### Deliverable 2 — Current State Architecture Diagram

**Commit to:** `/architecture/current-state.drawio` and `/architecture/current-state.png`

Use Draw.io (free at [app.diagrams.net](https://app.diagrams.net)). Show: DC1 London and DC2 Manchester as separate domains, server tiers (DMZ, App, Data, Infra), MPLS connectivity, external connections, and all 5 Oracle instances with their host servers.

---

### Deliverable 3 — Application Inventory Assessment

**Commit to:** `/migration/application-inventory.xlsx`

Complete the inventory with your 6R classification for all 28 apps, migration wave assignment, complexity rating (LOW / MEDIUM / HIGH / VERY HIGH), and confidence rating. Flag where you need more information.

---

### Deliverable 4 — Stakeholder Register

**Commit to:** `/docs/stakeholder-register.md`

For all 6 named stakeholders: name, role, interest level, influence level, key concern, communication preference, and potential conflict or blocker risk.

---

### Anti-Fake Validation

Your submission must reference specific data from the CSV files. Generic answers score zero. Required specifics:

- Exact count of EOL OS servers with server IDs

- Exact count of CRITICAL patch status servers

- Your MSSQL-03 finding and recommendation

- Your APP-027 recommendation with reasoning

- SRV-086 and SRV-087 risk classification

- At least one specific dependency chain cited from dependency-map.csv

- The SRV-076 shared Oracle instance finding (ORCL-04 and ORCL-05)

rubric.criterion_label: Infrastructure analysis — EOL OS count correct with server IDs, CRITICAL patch servers identified, SRV-086/087 assessed, SRV-076 shared Oracle documented
Application 6R classification — all 28 apps classified, APP-027 investigated with definitive recommendation
Dependency analysis — MSSQL-03 mystery resolved with recommendation, specific dependency chain cited from CSV
Risk register — 5 or more new risks with impact, likelihood, owner, and mitigation
Executive summary — board-readable, honest about timeline, specific risk calls with CSV evidence

content.text: Upload your Discovery Assessment Report (PDF or Markdown) and your Current State Architecture Diagram (.drawio or .png).

rubric.criterion_label: Addresses all four messy reality items (SRV-086/087, APP-027, MSSQL-03, SRV-076) with specific findings from the CSV data
Quantifies the EOL OS and CRITICAL patch risk with specific server IDs and regulatory implications

content.text:
Answer the four specific questions raised by Sarah and Michael:

**1.** What are SRV-086 and SRV-087? What is the risk? What action do you recommend?

**2.** Is APP-027 (Legacy Fund Accounting) still in use? What is your definitive recommendation?

**3.** What is MSSQL-03 and what should be done about it?

**4.** What is the significance of SRV-076 hosting both ORCL-04 and ORCL-05, and how does this affect the migration plan?

content.headline: Design the AWS environment that Tilington will migrate into — and make it regulator-proof.
content.description: Discovery is complete and approved. Now design the multi-account AWS structure, governance guardrails, and identity model. The Architecture Review Board is confirmed for 14 April. Emma Clarke wants written confirmation — not verbal — that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario.

content.text:
## Target AWS Architecture & Landing Zone

**Portfolio output:** Enterprise AWS Landing Zone Design

**GitHub evidence:** /architecture/target-state.drawio, /docs/hld.md, /docs/lld.md, /docs/adrs/ADR-003.md through ADR-005.md, /docs/governance-framework.md

**Tools:** AWS Free Tier (Organizations, Control Tower), Draw.io, GitHub

**Estimated time:** 8–12 hours

---

## Key Design Requirements

The architecture must support:

- Minimum 6 AWS accounts (Management, Security, Logging, Shared Services, Production, Non-Production)

- Service Control Policies blocking public S3 buckets, disabling CloudTrail, and PII leaving eu-west-2

- IAM Identity Centre with Azure AD federation — Tilington uses Microsoft 365

- Control Tower guardrails from Day 1

- Written technical mechanism answering Emma Clarke's data residency question — this must appear in the LLD, not just the ARB deck

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — High-Level Design (HLD)

**Commit to:** `/docs/hld.md`

Cover: solution overview with architecture principles applied, AWS account structure (minimum 6 accounts with OU hierarchy), SCP strategy, identity and access model (IAM Identity Centre + Azure AD federation, 6 roles), and governance controls.

Write at least 3 SCPs in actual JSON:

- Deny leaving eu-west-2 for services handling PII

- Deny creating S3 buckets with public access

- Deny disabling CloudTrail

### Deliverable 2 — Low-Level Design (LLD)

**Commit to:** `/docs/lld.md`

Specific implementable detail: per-account service list and SCPs applied, IAM role policies with trust conditions for at least one cross-account role, and the specific technical mechanism that answers Emma Clarke's data residency question.

### Deliverable 3 — Architecture Decision Records

**Commit to:** `/docs/adrs/ADR-003.md`, `/docs/adrs/ADR-004.md`, `/docs/adrs/ADR-005.md`

Follow the Nygard format (Context, Decision, Consequences, Alternatives Considered):

- **ADR-003** — eu-west-2 as primary region (UK GDPR rationale)

- **ADR-004** — Terraform as primary IaC (vs CloudFormation-only)

- **ADR-005** — EKS for container orchestration (EKS vs ECS trade-off — Tilington-specific, not generic)

### Deliverable 4 — Landing Zone Architecture Diagram

**Commit to:** `/architecture/target-state.drawio` and `.png`

Show: Organisation root → OUs → Accounts, Control Tower in management account, each account with primary services, cross-account trust relationships.

### Deliverable 5 — Governance Framework

**Commit to:** `/docs/governance-framework.md`

Cover: change management process, IaC-only policy (what constitutes ClickOps and what happens), security exception process, cost governance thresholds, and continuous compliance monitoring.

---

### Anti-Fake Validation

- SCPs must be written in valid JSON — describing them in prose scores zero

- ADR-005 (EKS vs ECS) must include Tilington-specific trade-offs

- Emma Clarke's data residency answer must name a specific AWS control mechanism

rubric.criterion_label: Account structure — 6+ accounts, OU hierarchy documented, rationale for each separation
SCPs in valid JSON — minimum 3, correct Effect/Action/Resource structure
Identity model — IAM Identity Centre with Azure AD federation, 6-role hierarchy defined
ADRs — minimum 3 in Nygard format with Tilington-specific trade-offs, not generic statements
Data residency — written technical mechanism naming the specific AWS control

content.text: Upload your HLD, LLD, and Landing Zone Architecture Diagram.

sections.title: Your SCPs and Data Residency Answer

rubric.criterion_label: 3 SCPs in valid JSON syntax with correct Effect, Action, Resource structure
Data residency answer names specific AWS controls — not prose assertions

content.text:
**Part 1 — Your 3 SCPs in JSON**

Paste your three Service Control Policies here in valid JSON.

**Part 2 — Data Residency Written Confirmation for Emma Clarke**

Emma's question: *Confirm that no customer PII will leave eu-west-2 under any scenario, including DR failover to eu-west-1. What technical controls enforce this? What happens if the control fails?*

Answer with specific AWS mechanisms only — not prose assertions.

content.headline: Design the network that connects Tilington's datacentres to AWS and the security controls that keep the FCA satisfied.
content.description: The Landing Zone is approved. Now design the network, complete the Terraform networking module, and respond to two security incidents that landed in the January findings report. There is also a cost trap in the January AWS report — it looks like a monitoring problem but it is actually an architectural issue with a free fix.

content.text:
## Networking, Security & Hybrid Cloud

**Portfolio output:** Enterprise Security & Network Architecture

**GitHub evidence:** /architecture/networking.drawio, /docs/security-architecture.md, /security/compliance-mapping.md, /terraform/networking/main.tf (completed)

**Tools:** AWS VPC, Transit Gateway, IAM, KMS, GuardDuty, Security Hub, WAF, Terraform, Draw.io

**Estimated time:** 8–12 hours

---

## The Cost Trap

From the January AWS cost report:

- CloudWatch DataProcessing: $89.40 — looks like the problem

- AWS Data Transfer Regional: $156.30 — this is the real architectural issue

The fix is two free VPC gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB. Annual saving: $1,875.60. Candidates who identify CloudWatch as the root cause are wrong.

---

## Active Security Incidents

SF-002 and SF-018 require immediate response — a production S3 bucket is publicly accessible and Macie has detected National Insurance numbers inside it. Emma Clarke has invoked the 72-hour ICO notification clock.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Complete the Terraform Networking Module

**Commit to:** `/terraform/networking/main.tf` (complete all TODOs)

Required additions: private application subnets (3 AZs), private data subnets (3 AZs), NAT Gateway(s) with HA vs cost ADR, route tables (public → IGW, private → NAT GW), VPC Flow Logs, and security groups for ALB, EKS nodes, and Aurora — all least-privilege.

**VPC CIDR must not conflict with:** DC1 (10.10.0.0/16), DC2 (10.20.0.0/16), non-prod (10.40.0.0/16).

Fix SF-004: port 22 open to 0.0.0.0/0 on EKS node security group — replace SSH with AWS SSM Session Manager.

### Deliverable 2 — Network Architecture Diagram

**Commit to:** `/architecture/networking.drawio` and `.png`

Show: DC1 and DC2 connectivity to AWS (Direct Connect vs VPN — justify your recommendation citing the $380/month Direct Connect cost), VPC with 3 tiers across 3 AZs, Transit Gateway, VPC gateway endpoints for S3 and DynamoDB, Route 53 private hosted zones.

### Deliverable 3 — Security Architecture Document

**Commit to:** `/docs/security-architecture.md`

Configure each required service: CloudTrail, Config, GuardDuty, Security Hub, KMS, WAF, Macie. Define KMS CMK strategy (how many keys, what purpose, rotation policy). Map all 14 NCSC Cloud Security Principles to specific AWS controls — no blanks permitted.

### Deliverable 4 — Compliance Mapping

**Commit to:** `/security/compliance-mapping.md`

Complete the NCSC 14 principles mapping. Add ISO 27001 domains A.9, A.10, A.12, A.13 mapped to AWS controls.

### Deliverable 5 — Cross-AZ Cost Analysis

From the January cost report:

- CloudWatch DataProcessing: $89.40

- AWS Data Transfer Regional: $156.30

One is a monitoring verbosity issue. One is an architectural problem with a free fix. Identify which is which, name the root cause, calculate the annual saving.

### Deliverable 6 — Incident Response: SF-002 and SF-018

Respond to Emma Clarke's urgent message. Provide: public access block confirmation, ICO Article 33 assessment (72-hour clock), data lineage explanation, and the governance fix that prevents recurrence.

rubric.criterion_label: Private subnets, NAT GW, route tables, VPC flow logs completed — no 0.0.0.0/0 ingress except ALB
All 14 NCSC principles mapped to specific AWS controls — no blanks
Data Transfer Regional ($156.30) correctly identified as architectural root cause — not CloudWatch. VPC gateway endpoints named as free fix with $1,875.60 annual saving
SF-002/SF-018 response: ICO Article 33 assessment, data lineage, governance fix named

content.text: Paste your completed `/terraform/networking/main.tf` here with all TODOs resolved.

rubric.criterion_label: Correctly identifies Data Transfer Regional ($156.30) as the architectural problem — not CloudWatch ($89.40). Explains why.
Names S3 gateway endpoint and DynamoDB gateway endpoint as the free fix, calculates $1,875.60 annual saving
Emma Clarke response: ICO Article 33 72-hour clock addressed, data lineage explained, governance fix named

content.text:
**Part 1 — Cross-AZ Cost Analysis**

Which line item is the real architectural problem and why? What is the free fix? Calculate the annual saving.

**Part 2 — Response to Emma Clarke (SF-002 / SF-018)**

Address: public access block confirmation, ICO Article 33 notification assessment, data lineage explanation, and the governance fix.

content.headline: Build the infrastructure. Write working code. Resolve three live incidents.
content.description: The architecture and network are approved. Now build it. Complete the Terraform modules, Python automation tools, and CloudFormation templates — and resolve three live operational incidents. Every deliverable in this phase requires working code committed to GitHub.

content.text:
## Infrastructure as Code, Automation & Operations

**Portfolio output:** Infrastructure Automation Platform

**GitHub evidence:** /terraform/ (all modules complete and plan-clean), /cloudformation/vpc.yaml (complete), /cloudformation/security.yaml (new), /python/inventory-tool/inventory.py (complete + sample-output.csv), /python/cost-reporting-tool/cost_report.py (complete), /docs/incident-resolution-report.md, /docs/operations-runbook.md

**Tools:** Terraform, CloudFormation, Python, Boto3, AWS CLI, GitHub, VS Code

**Estimated time:** 10–15 hours

---

## Three Incidents Awaiting You

**Incident 1 — SSH Brute Force (SF-006):** 847 failed SSH attempts in 10 minutes from a known Tor exit node targeting SRV-086. Root cause, containment, and long-term fix required.

**Incident 2 — Cross-AZ Cost Anomaly:** Data Transfer Regional at $156.30/month. Root cause: services crossing AZs to reach S3 and DynamoDB without VPC endpoints. Fix: free gateway endpoints. Annual saving: $1,875.60.

**Incident 3 — Untagged EC2 (i-0nonprod003):** $98.40/month, no owner, no application tag. Investigate, resolve, and implement the governance fix.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Complete Terraform Repository

**Commit to:** `/terraform/` (all three modules)

**Networking module** — must `terraform plan` with 0 errors. Remote state in S3. All outputs populated.

**Security/IAM module** — tighten KMS key policy from root wildcard to specific principals. Add S3 Object Lock in **COMPLIANCE mode** on CloudTrail bucket (not GOVERNANCE — explain the difference in a comment). Add S3 bucket policy denying all puts except from CloudTrail service.

**EKS module** — add OIDC provider for IRSA, at least one managed node group, aws-load-balancer-controller addon.

Include `terraform plan` output as evidence in your submission.

### Deliverable 2 — Python Inventory Tool

**Commit to:** `/python/inventory-tool/inventory.py` and `/python/inventory-tool/sample-output.csv`

Complete all TODO functions:

- `get_rds_inventory()` — must identify Oracle instances (engine `oracle-ee` or `oracle-se2`)

- `get_s3_inventory()` — must flag unencrypted and public buckets as HIGH severity

- `get_eks_inventory()` — must flag public API server endpoints

Run the tool and commit actual CSV output.

### Deliverable 3 — Python Cost Reporting Tool

**Commit to:** `/python/cost-reporting-tool/cost_report.py`

Complete `get_cost_by_service()` and `get_cost_by_tag()`. Your analysis must identify Data Transfer Regional ($156.30/month) as the architectural problem — not CloudWatch ($89.40). Fix with VPC gateway endpoints. Annual saving: $1,875.60.

### Deliverable 4 — CloudFormation Templates

**Commit to:** `/cloudformation/vpc.yaml` (complete), `/cloudformation/security.yaml` (new)

Complete vpc.yaml (all subnets, NAT GW, routes). Create security.yaml enabling Security Hub with FSBP and CIS standards, and GuardDuty.

### Deliverable 5 — Three Incident Reports

**Commit to:** `/docs/incident-resolution-report.md`

**Incident 1 — SSH Brute Force (SF-006):** 847 failed attempts in 10 minutes from a Tor exit node. Root cause, containment, long-term fix (SSM Session Manager replacing SSH), evidence.

**Incident 2 — Cross-AZ Cost Anomaly:** Data Transfer Regional $156.30/month is architectural. Fix with VPC gateway endpoints (free). Annual saving $1,875.60. Evidence.

**Incident 3 — Untagged EC2 i-0nonprod003 ($98.40/month):** Investigation, owner identified or instance terminated, governance fix — name the SCP or Config rule that prevents recurrence.

### Deliverable 6 — Operations Runbook

**Commit to:** `/docs/operations-runbook.md`

Cover: EKS node drain and rolling update, Aurora failover and restore from snapshot, Terraform safe apply and state drift recovery, incident escalation path at Tilington.

rubric.criterion_label: terraform plan output shows 0 errors across all three modules
CloudTrail S3 uses Object Lock COMPLIANCE mode with explanation of difference from GOVERNANCE
Python inventory tool produces actual CSV output with Oracle instances (oracle-ee/oracle-se2) correctly identified
security.yaml creates Security Hub with FSBP and CIS standards enabled plus GuardDuty detector
All 3 incidents resolved: root cause, containment, long-term fix, evidence — untagged instance governance fix named

content.text: Paste your `terraform plan` output showing 0 errors across all three modules.

rubric.criterion_label: SSH brute force: root cause identified, SSM Session Manager named as the long-term fix replacing SSH
Cross-AZ cost: Data Transfer Regional correctly identified, VPC endpoints named, $1,875.60 annual saving calculated
Untagged instance: investigation documented, governance fix (SCP or Config rule) named

content.text:
Document all three incidents. For each:

**Root Cause** — what caused this issue?

**Immediate Action** — what did you do to contain it?

**Long-term Fix** — what prevents recurrence?

**Evidence** — what confirms it is resolved?

content.headline: Decompose Tilington's legacy customer platform into four cloud-native microservices and deploy them to EKS.
content.description: The IaC platform is operational. Now decompose Tilington's legacy customer platform into four cloud-native microservices and deploy them to EKS using GitOps. The Payments Service is PCI DSS scope. The Audit Service must have no TTL — FCA audit records cannot be automatically deleted.

content.text:
## Kubernetes, Microservices & GitOps

**Portfolio output:** Cloud-Native Microservices Platform

**GitHub evidence:** /kubernetes/ (all 4 services with deployment, service, hpa, pdb, serviceaccount), /gitops/argocd/applicationset.yaml, /gitops/overlays/production/ and /non-production/, /architecture/microservices.drawio, /docs/service-boundaries.md, /docs/api-design.md

**Tools:** Docker Desktop, kubectl, Helm, ArgoCD, Draw.io, GitHub, VS Code

**Estimated time:** 12–18 hours

---

## The Four Microservices

## Your Tasks

### The Four Microservices

---

### Deliverable 1 — Kubernetes Manifests for All 4 Services

**Commit to:** `/kubernetes/` (one directory per service)

For each microservice create: `deployment.yaml`, `service.yaml`, `hpa.yaml`, `pdb.yaml`, `serviceaccount.yaml`.

Every deployment must include security context (`runAsNonRoot: true`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation: false`), topology spread constraints across 3 AZs, resource limits and requests, liveness and readiness probes, and IRSA annotation (`eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn`) on ServiceAccount.

Payments Service: separate namespace or NetworkPolicy restricting inbound to ALB and Customer Service only.

### Deliverable 2 — Helm Chart Templates

**Commit to:** `/kubernetes/helm/tilington-services/templates/`

Create: `deployment.yaml`, `service.yaml`, `hpa.yaml`, `ingress.yaml`, `serviceaccount.yaml`, `pdb.yaml`.

The chart must pass `helm template .` without errors — paste output as evidence.

### Deliverable 3 — ArgoCD GitOps Configuration

**Commit to:** `/gitops/argocd/applicationset.yaml`, `/gitops/overlays/production/kustomization.yaml`, `/gitops/overlays/non-production/kustomization.yaml`

Fix `application.yaml` with real EKS cluster URL format. Create an ApplicationSet iterating over all 4 microservices.

### Deliverable 4 — Service Boundary and API Design

**Commit to:** `/docs/service-boundaries.md`, `/docs/api-design.md`

For each service: what data it owns, reads via API, events it publishes, events it subscribes to.

Customer Service REST API: method, path, request/response JSON schema, HTTP status codes, PII fields flagged.

sections.title: Submit Your Helm Chart and ArgoCD Config

rubric.criterion_label: All 4 deployments have correct security context (runAsNonRoot, readOnlyRootFilesystem, allowPrivilegeEscalation: false), topology spread, and IRSA annotation
helm template produces valid YAML without errors — output pasted as evidence
ArgoCD ApplicationSet iterates over all 4 microservices, production and non-production overlays both created
Payments Service PCI isolation implemented via separate namespace or NetworkPolicy

content.text: Paste your `helm template .` output proving the chart produces valid YAML. Then paste your ArgoCD ApplicationSet YAML.

rubric.criterion_label: Explains PCI DSS isolation for Payments Service with the specific Kubernetes mechanism used
Explains why AuditTrail DynamoDB must have NO TTL and names the FCA regulatory basis
Explains IRSA and why every ServiceAccount needs the eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn annotation

content.text:
Answer these three questions:

**1.** How have you isolated the Payments Service for PCI DSS compliance? What specific Kubernetes mechanism?

**2.** The Audit Service writes to DynamoDB. Why must this table have NO TTL? What FCA regulation requires this and what happens if you set a TTL?

**3.** What is IRSA and why does every microservice ServiceAccount need the `eks.amazonaws.com/role-arn` annotation?

content.headline: Fix a broken pipeline, build a secure delivery process, and define the SLOs that prove FCA operational resilience.
content.description: The microservices platform is deployed. Now build the delivery pipeline and observability stack. The GitHub Actions pipeline has 10 documented bugs — find and fix all of them. BUG-004 is the most subtle. You will also define SLOs and calculate error budgets that Emma Clarke will use as FCA operational resilience evidence.

content.text:
## CI/CD, DevSecOps & Observability

**Portfolio output:** Enterprise Delivery & Reliability Platform

**GitHub evidence:** /.github/workflows/deploy.yml (fixed — all 10 bugs), /ci-cd/security-scan-results/ (Trivy JSON + Checkov txt), /observability/grafana/dashboards/ (3 dashboard JSON exports), /docs/devsecops-pipeline.md, /docs/reliability-runbook.md

**Tools:** GitHub Actions, Trivy, Checkov, CloudWatch, Grafana Cloud (free at grafana.com/products/cloud), Prometheus

**Estimated time:** 10–15 hours

---

## BUG-004 — The Subtle One

The security-scan job is defined. It appears to run. But it never actually blocks the pipeline.

The root cause: when a GitHub Actions job is skipped due to an `if:` condition evaluating to false on a non-main branch, it returns a status of `success` — not `skipped`. A downstream job with `needs: security-scan` sees success and proceeds regardless.

Fix: remove the `if:` condition from the security-scan job so it runs on every branch.

---

## SLO Error Budget — Payments Service

Payments Service SLO: 99.95% availability over 30 days.

Error budget = 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = 21.6 minutes per month. Show your working.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Fix All 10 Pipeline Bugs

**Commit to:** `/.github/workflows/deploy.yml`

For each bug: root cause, specific lines changed, explanation of why the original failed.

### Deliverable 2 — Security Scanning Evidence

**Commit to:** `/ci-cd/security-scan-results/`

Run Trivy against a real Docker image — commit JSON output to `trivy-scan-output.json`. Run Checkov against Kubernetes manifests — commit to `checkov-k8s-scan.txt`. For each FAILED Checkov check: document what it flagged, why it matters, what you changed.

### Deliverable 3 — Grafana Cloud Dashboards

**Commit to:** `/observability/grafana/dashboards/`

Create 3 dashboards in Grafana Cloud free tier (grafana.com/products/cloud). Export each as JSON.

- **Dashboard 1** — Service Health: request rate, error rate, P95 latency for all 4 services

- **Dashboard 2** — Reliability and SLO: SLO compliance, error budget remaining, burn rate

- **Dashboard 3** — EKS Infrastructure: node CPU/memory, pod scheduling, HPA events

### Deliverable 4 — SLO Framework

**Commit to:** `/docs/reliability-runbook.md`

Define SLOs for all 4 services. Calculate the Payments Service error budget: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = **21.6 minutes/month** — show your working. Define fast burn alert and slow burn alert thresholds. Define escalation path. Explain how this provides FCA PS21/3 evidence.

rows: docker build uses wrong context path
No Trivy scan on built image
security-scan job never blocks — needs:/if: skipped-job interaction (most subtle)
AWS credentials via long-lived access keys — replace with OIDC
deploy-production has no AWS credentials step
content.caption: For each bug: identify the root cause, show the specific lines changed, and explain why the original code failed.
Payments Service error budget: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = 21.6 minutes per month. Show your working.

rubric.criterion_label: All 10 bugs fixed with root cause and lines changed — BUG-004 correctly explains needs:/if: skipped-job interaction
BUG-005 fixed with OIDC role-to-assume — no long-lived credentials in GitHub secrets
BUG-008 rollback added via kubectl rollout undo, BUG-009 smoke test checks /health/ready endpoint
Real Trivy scan output committed (not mocked), Checkov failed checks documented with remediation

content.text: Paste your fixed `deploy.yml` with each BUG-001 through BUG-010 annotated showing what was wrong and what was changed.

rubric.criterion_label: Payments Service error budget correctly calculated as 21.6 minutes/month with working shown (30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005)
Fast burn and slow burn thresholds defined with specific percentages and time windows
SLO framework explicitly connected to FCA PS21/3 — explains what Emma Clarke would show the regulator

content.text:
**SLO Error Budget Calculations**

Calculate the error budget for all 4 services. Show your working for the Payments Service: 30 × 24 × 60 × 0.0005 = ?

**Alert Thresholds**

Define fast burn alert (triggers a page) and slow burn alert (triggers a ticket) with specific thresholds and time windows.

**FCA PS21/3 Connection**

How does this SLO framework provide evidence for FCA operational resilience compliance? What would Emma Clarke show the FCA regulator?

content.headline: Design the data layer that replaces Oracle, serves the microservices, and keeps Tilington FCA-compliant.
content.description: The platform is live. Now design the data layer — Aurora PostgreSQL to replace Oracle, DynamoDB for event data, and the migration path. The Oracle renewal deadline is Q2 2026. ORCL-01 has 847 stored procedures, 214 requiring manual conversion. Two constraints are non-negotiable: AuditTrail has no TTL, and Aurora customer data cannot replicate to eu-west-1.

content.text:
## Database Architecture

**Portfolio output:** Cloud-Native Database Architecture

**GitHub evidence:** /databases/aurora-design.md (complete), /databases/dynamodb-design.md (complete — all 3 tables), /architecture/database.drawio, /docs/backup-recovery-strategy.md

**Tools:** Aurora PostgreSQL, DynamoDB, Draw.io, GitHub

**Estimated time:** 8–12 hours

---

## Non-Negotiable Constraints

**AuditTrail DynamoDB — NO TTL.** FCA COBS record-keeping means some audit records must be retained indefinitely. Setting a TTL is a compliance failure.

**Aurora customer data — cannot replicate to eu-west-1.** UK GDPR. Your backup and DR strategy must explicitly address this.

**Payments Service RPO — 15 minutes.** Daily automated backups give 24-hour RPO. Aurora PITR gives sub-minute RPO. Only PITR achieves 15 minutes.

---

## Oracle Renewal Decision

Sarah needs a specific recommendation — renew or do not renew. Not a risk matrix. A decision. ORCL-01, ORCL-02, and ORCL-03 are the Q2 2026 migration candidates. Quantify the £4.2M three-year saving.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Complete Aurora PostgreSQL Design

**Commit to:** `/databases/aurora-design.md`

Complete all TODOs: instance class selection justified against ORCL-01 workload (not SRV-006 server specs — Aurora manages memory differently). Complete SQL schema for customers, accounts, and transactions tables. Oracle migration specifics: list PL/SQL features in `customer_ledger_pkg` requiring manual conversion, DMS task configuration for ORCL-01 to Aurora. Backup retention with FCA record-keeping justification.

### Deliverable 2 — Complete DynamoDB Design — All 3 Tables

**Commit to:** `/databases/dynamodb-design.md`

**PaymentEvents** — resolve the hot partition problem on the status GSI. The PENDING partition will receive all writes — propose write sharding or an alternative concrete solution.

**NotificationDelivery** — design from scratch: partition key, sort key, 2 GSIs with query patterns, TTL set to 90 days.

**AuditTrail** — design from scratch with **NO TTL** (FCA COBS record-keeping — some audit records must be retained indefinitely). Separate KMS key `alias/tilington-audit`. Access model: audit-service write-only, compliance-viewer read-only.

### Deliverable 3 — Backup and Recovery Strategy

**Commit to:** `/docs/backup-recovery-strategy.md`

Aurora: backup retention with FCA justification. Payments Service needs 15-minute RPO — Aurora automated backups alone do not achieve this. **Aurora PITR does** — continuously backs up to S3, enabling restore to any second within the retention period.

Cross-region constraint: Aurora customer data cannot replicate to eu-west-1. Propose the solution.

DynamoDB: PITR for AuditTrail. No Global Tables for AuditTrail (UK data residency).

### Deliverable 4 — Oracle Renewal Recommendation

**One-page recommendation for Sarah Williams:**

Name ORCL-01, ORCL-02, ORCL-03 as the Q2 2026 migration candidates. Address Michael's PL/SQL warning honestly (847 stored procedures, 214 requiring manual conversion). Give a **specific recommendation** — renew or do not renew. Quantify: £1.4M/year Oracle cost, £4.2M saving over 3 years if not renewed.

---

### Key Design Constraints

- AuditTrail DynamoDB: **NO TTL** — FCA COBS record-keeping means some audit records must be retained indefinitely. Setting a TTL is a compliance failure.

- Aurora customer data: **cannot replicate to eu-west-1** — UK GDPR. Your DR strategy must explicitly address this constraint.

- Payments Service RPO: **15 minutes** — this requires PITR, not daily automated backups.

rows: NO TTL — table must retain records indefinitely
Cannot replicate customer PII to eu-west-1
15-minute RPO requires Aurora PITR — not daily backups

rubric.criterion_label: Aurora instance sizing justified against ORCL-01 workload not SRV-006 server specs, SQL schema complete with indexes
PaymentEvents hot partition solved with concrete mechanism — write sharding or equivalent proposed
AuditTrail has NO TTL with FCA COBS justification, separate KMS key, correct least-privilege access model
Payments Service 15-minute RPO achieved via Aurora PITR specifically — daily backups not sufficient, correctly distinguished
Oracle recommendation names ORCL-01/02/03, addresses PL/SQL complexity, gives specific recommendation with £4.2M saving

content.text:
Provide your key database design decisions here:

**Aurora PostgreSQL** — instance class selection with justification. Key schema decisions for customer_ledger_pkg migration.

**DynamoDB PaymentEvents** — how did you resolve the hot partition problem? Describe your concrete solution.

**DynamoDB AuditTrail** — confirm NO TTL and explain the FCA regulatory basis.

**Payments Service RPO** — how does PITR achieve 15-minute RPO? Why don't daily automated backups achieve this?

**Oracle Recommendation** — renew or do not renew? Your specific recommendation with reasoning and the £4.2M saving quantified.

content.headline: Plan the migration, design DR, and validate the programme is on budget.
content.description: The platform is built and databases are designed. Now plan how 28 applications migrate across 5 waves, design the disaster recovery architecture, and produce a FinOps assessment from real cost data. Sum the January spend from the CSV — do not estimate. One line item is a $1,875.60/year free fix hiding in plain sight.

content.text:
## Migration, Disaster Recovery & FinOps

**Portfolio output:** Cloud Migration, DR & FinOps Programme

**GitHub evidence:** /migration/migration-strategy.md (complete), /migration/migration-waves.xlsx, /architecture/dr.drawio, /docs/rto-rpo-matrix.md, /finops/finops-assessment.md, /docs/well-architected-review.md

**Tools:** AWS Cost Explorer, Excel or Google Sheets, Draw.io, GitHub

**Estimated time:** 10–15 hours

---

## The DR Constraint

AuditTrail DynamoDB must NOT appear in the DR replication plan. FCA audit records must remain in eu-west-2 (UK jurisdiction) only. This is the same data residency constraint that prevents Aurora customer data from replicating to eu-west-1.

---

## APP-012 is Critical Path

25 applications depend on the Authentication Service (APP-012). It must be migrated in Wave 2 — before any customer-facing or business-critical applications. Candidates who assign it to a later wave will have an impossible dependency chain.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — Complete Migration Strategy

**Commit to:** `/migration/migration-strategy.md`

Assign all 28 apps to a wave with reasoning. APP-012 (Authentication Service) must be Wave 2 — 25 applications depend on it. Define entry and exit criteria for all 5 waves. Resolve every app with `???` in the wave candidates CSV.

### Deliverable 2 — FinOps Assessment

**Commit to:** `/finops/finops-assessment.md`

**Calculate the total January spend** by summing the `blended_cost_usd` column — do not estimate, sum the actual data.

Identify and quantify all 5 specific findings:

1. Aurora writer `db.r6g.2xlarge` at $2,180.16/month — right-sized? Compare to ORCL-01 server (SRV-006: 32 vCPU, 256GB) — Aurora manages memory differently, justify your conclusion.

2. Direct Connect at $380/month — cost justified against £56,667/month programme budget?

3. Non-prod EKS nodes running 24/7 — 2 nodes at $28.08/month each. Saving if scheduled off nights/weekends: $28.08 × 2 × (5/7) ≈ $40/month.

4. Untagged instance i-0nonprod003 at $98.40/month — governance issue.

5. Cross-AZ transfer $156.30/month — free fix via VPC gateway endpoints. Annual saving: $1,875.60.

### Deliverable 3 — DR Architecture

**Commit to:** `/architecture/dr.drawio` and `.png`

Primary: eu-west-2. DR: eu-west-1 (NO customer PII). Aurora: PITR snapshots only — no live PII replica in eu-west-1. **DynamoDB AuditTrail: excluded from replication entirely** — FCA audit records must stay in UK jurisdiction. Route 53 health checks and failover. EKS warm or cold standby.

### Deliverable 4 — RTO/RPO Matrix

**Commit to:** `/docs/rto-rpo-matrix.md`

For each of the 4 services: FCA requirement, target achieved, AWS mechanism, any gap with remediation. Payments Service 15-minute RPO must cite Aurora PITR specifically.

### Deliverable 5 — AWS Well-Architected Review

**Commit to:** `/docs/well-architected-review.md`

Self-assess all 5 pillars. Minimum 2 evidence-based risks per pillar from the security findings CSV and cost report. Include remediation for each.

content.headline: Defend your full AWS architecture in front of the Board. Answer the CRO's questions in writing. Leave with a portfolio that gets you hired.
content.description: The full technical programme is complete. Now defend your architecture at the Architecture Review Board. Emma Clarke has sent three specific questions that must be answered in writing with named technical mechanisms — not prose. The CFO will ask about Oracle savings and the January spend total.

content.text:
## Architecture Review Board & Executive Presentation

**Portfolio output:** Executive AWS Architecture Portfolio

**GitHub evidence:** /presentations/arb-presentation.pptx, /docs/architecture-decision-log.md, /docs/final-risk-summary.md, /docs/final-portfolio-summary.md

**Tools:** PowerPoint or Google Slides, Draw.io, GitHub

**Estimated time:** 8–12 hours

---

## Emma Clarke's Three Questions

These must be answered in writing in the ARB pack — not verbally on the day.

**Q1 — Data residency during DR failover to eu-west-1:** Name the SCP preventing eu-west-1 PII use, the Aurora snapshot-only strategy, the DynamoDB AuditTrail exclusion, and the CloudTrail detective control.

**Q2 — Operational resilience during an AWS eu-west-2 availability event:** Cover Multi-AZ within eu-west-2, eu-west-1 warm standby, Route 53 health check failover, and in-flight payment handling during failover.

**Q3 — Administrative access to the AWS Production account:** Name specific IAM Identity Centre roles, the break-glass procedure, CloudTrail audit coverage, and the monthly access review cadence.

---

## ARB Panel

Emma Clarke (CRO), Priya Sharma (Head of Information Security), David Okafor (Head of Retail Technology), and the CFO.

## Your Tasks

### Deliverable 1 — ARB Presentation Pack (12–16 slides)

**Commit to:** `/presentations/arb-presentation.pptx`

Required slides: programme title, executive summary, AWS architecture overview, multi-account structure and governance, security posture (Security Hub findings closed, NCSC compliance), written answer to Emma Clarke Q1 on data residency, operational resilience with RTO/RPO matrix, migration wave plan status, FinOps showing January spend total from CSV and Oracle saving of £4.2M, outstanding risks, and next steps to DC exit.

### Deliverable 2 — Emma Clarke's Three Written Answers

**Every answer must name specific technical mechanisms — not prose assertions.**

**Q1 — Data residency during DR failover to eu-west-1:**

Name: the SCP preventing eu-west-1 PII use, the Aurora snapshot-only strategy, the DynamoDB AuditTrail exclusion from replication, and the CloudTrail detective control if the SCP is bypassed.

**Q2 — Operational resilience during an AWS eu-west-2 availability event:**

Cover: Multi-AZ within eu-west-2 (AZ failure handling), eu-west-1 warm standby (region failure), Route 53 health check failover mechanism, and what happens to in-flight payments during failover.

**Q3 — Administrative access to the AWS Production account:**

Name: specific IAM Identity Centre roles with Production account access, the break-glass procedure, CloudTrail coverage of all privileged actions, and the monthly access review cadence.

### Deliverable 3 — Architecture Decision Log

**Commit to:** `/docs/architecture-decision-log.md`

All ADRs from all phases in a single table: number, title, status, date, decided by, one-sentence summary. Note any ADRs revised or superseded.

### Deliverable 4 — Risk and Trade-Off Summary

**Commit to:** `/docs/final-risk-summary.md`

Document the 5 key trade-offs: NAT Gateway 1 vs 3 (cite ~£90/month per additional gateway), Aurora instance class vs Oracle server parity, Direct Connect vs VPN, rehost vs refactor timing for APP-002, public vs private EKS API endpoint.

### Deliverable 5 — Final Portfolio Summary

**Commit to:** `/docs/final-portfolio-summary.md`

One page suitable for a CV or LinkedIn project description. Name specific AWS services. Quantify outcomes. Map skills to UK financial services roles paying £70,000–£125,000.

Customer app stability, migration timeline, user impact
Budget vs actual, Oracle saving (£4.2M), ROI

content.summary_text:
You have completed the Tilington plc Cloud Modernisation Programme — a full enterprise AWS Solutions Architect simulation across 9 phases of real work: discovery, architecture, networking, IaC, Kubernetes, CI/CD, databases, migration, and executive governance.

Your GitHub repository at github.com/alain-Sortnext/tilington-cloud-modernisation now contains your complete portfolio — 23 deliverables demonstrating every skill required for mid-senior AWS Solutions Architect roles in UK financial services.
skills_earned: AWS Multi-Account Architecture — Organizations, Control Tower, SCPs
HLD and LLD Documentation for Enterprise AWS Workloads
VPC Design, Transit Gateway, and Hybrid Cloud Connectivity
IAM, KMS, GuardDuty, Security Hub — Regulated Security Architecture
Terraform IaC — Networking, Security, and EKS Modules
Kubernetes/EKS — Manifests, Helm, Autoscaling, Security Contexts
GitOps — ArgoCD ApplicationSet and Kustomize Overlays
GitHub Actions CI/CD with DevSecOps — Trivy, Checkov, OIDC
Observability — Grafana Cloud, Prometheus, SLO and Error Budget Design
Aurora PostgreSQL — Design, Oracle Migration via DMS, PITR
DynamoDB — Access Pattern Design and Hot Partition Resolution
Cloud Migration Strategy — 6Rs, Wave Planning, Cutover and Rollback
Disaster Recovery Architecture — RTO/RPO Design, FCA Operational Resilience
FinOps — Cost Explorer Analysis, Savings Plans, Cost Optimisation
Architecture Review Board Presentation and Executive Communication
FCA PS21/3, UK GDPR, PCI DSS, and NCSC 14 Principles Compliance
target_roles: AWS Solutions Architect at UK Tier-1 Bank — £75,000–£110,000 (Lloyds, HSBC, Barclays, NatWest)
Senior Cloud Architect at UK Insurance or Asset Management — £72,000–£95,000 (Aviva, Nationwide, Prudential)
Cloud Solutions Architect at UK FinTech — £90,000–£130,000 plus equity (Monzo, Starling, Revolut)
AWS Cloud Architect at Big-4 Consultancy — £80,000–£140,000 (Accenture, KPMG, Deloitte)
AWS Cloud Architect Contract — £600–£750 per day

items: Enterprise AWS architecture is never just technical. Every design decision at Tilington was driven by a regulatory constraint (FCA PS21/3, UK GDPR), a business deadline (Q3 2027 DC exit), or a stakeholder requirement (Emma Clarke's written data residency confirmation). Real architects start with constraints, not services.

The messy reality of legacy estates is the interview differentiator. Finding SRV-086 and SRV-087, resolving the MSSQL-03 mystery, identifying the ORCL-04 and ORCL-05 shared server on SRV-076, and investigating APP-027 demonstrate the discovery skills UK banks actually need — skills that cannot be faked with textbook knowledge.

IaC discipline is non-negotiable in regulated financial services. Every control Emma Clarke required — immutable CloudTrail logs, KMS CMKs, Object Lock in COMPLIANCE mode, tagged resources — must live in Terraform or CloudFormation. ClickOps in a regulated environment is an audit failure waiting to happen.

Cost anomalies are architectural problems, not monitoring problems. The January report showed CloudWatch DataProcessing at $89.40 as the apparent issue, but Data Transfer Regional at $156.30 was the real root cause. Free VPC gateway endpoints eliminated $1,875.60 per year. Real FinOps work requires reading the data correctly.

The completion phase of any cloud programme is governance, not technology. The ARB presentation, Emma Clarke's written answers, the architecture decision log, and the risk and trade-off summary are what regulators, boards, and senior stakeholders actually read. Building excellent infrastructure you cannot explain in board language is not enough.

skills: AWS Multi-Account Architecture and Landing Zone Design
Enterprise Security Architecture — FCA, PRA, NCSC Compliance
Infrastructure as Code — Terraform and CloudFormation
Kubernetes and EKS Platform Engineering with GitOps
Cloud-Native Database Architecture — Aurora PostgreSQL and DynamoDB
Cloud Migration Strategy — 6Rs and Wave Planning
Disaster Recovery Architecture and FCA Operational Resilience
Architecture Review Board Governance and Executive Communication
